proposed_hypotheses.text: Greater age_years is associated with longer pfs_months across the cohort.
Mean pfs_months differs between patients with sex_female=1 and sex_female=0.
Mean pfs_months differs between postmenopausal=1 and postmenopausal=0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Strong positive Pearson correlation between age_years and pfs_months (r=0.702, p<1e-300). Older patients have substantially longer PFS in this cohort.
Mean pfs_months 4.686 in females vs 4.699 in males; difference -0.013 mo (Welch t-test p=0.56).
Mean pfs_months 4.684 in postmenopausal vs 4.707 in pre-menopausal; difference -0.023 mo (p=0.31).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with stage_iv=1 have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0.
Patients with has_brain_mets=1 have shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0.
Patients with node_positive=1 have shorter pfs_months than node_positive=0.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (monotonic decrease across 0/1/2).
Larger tumor_size_cm is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher ki67_pct is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Stage IV patients have markedly shorter PFS (~3.45 vs 5.22 mo).
Brain mets patients have shorter PFS (~3.78 vs 4.80 mo).
Node-positive patients have slightly shorter PFS than node-negative (~4.61 vs 4.78 mo, modest).
ECOG 0/1/2 mean PFS = 5.64 / 4.44 / 3.29 mo; ANOVA F=2860.7, p<1e-300. Strong monotonic decrease.
Tumor size has trivial correlation with PFS (r=-0.005, p=0.30) when looked at alone.
Ki67% negatively correlated with PFS (r=-0.165, p<1e-300).
Weight loss negatively correlated with PFS (r=-0.125, p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with er_positive=1 have longer pfs_months than er_positive=0.
Patients with pr_positive=1 have longer pfs_months than pr_positive=0.
Patients with her2_positive=1 have shorter pfs_months than her2_positive=0.
Patients with her2_low=1 have different pfs_months than those without.
Patients with brca1_mutation=1 have different pfs_months than those without.
Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have different pfs_months than those without.
Patients with pik3ca_mutation=1 have shorter pfs_months than those without.

analyses.result_summary: ER+ have longer PFS (4.86 vs 4.30 mo, +0.57 mo, p=3.7e-143).
PR+ have longer PFS (4.82 vs 4.46 mo, +0.36 mo, p=5.8e-59).
HER2+ have shorter PFS (4.32 vs 4.78 mo, -0.46 mo, p=6.1e-70).
HER2-low slightly longer PFS (4.76 vs 4.65 mo, +0.11 mo, p=1e-6).
BRCA1 mutation: PFS 4.67 vs 4.69 mo, no significant difference (p=0.72).
BRCA2 mutation: PFS 4.57 vs 4.70 mo, p=0.08 (trend).
PIK3CA-mutant patients have shorter PFS (4.33 vs 4.89 mo, -0.56 mo, p=6e-144).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Albumin positively correlated with PFS (r=0.100, p<1e-100).
LDH negatively but weakly correlated with PFS (r=-0.016, p=2.4e-4).
CRP shows no marginal association with PFS (r=-0.003, p=0.46).
NLR shows no marginal association with PFS (r=0.003, p=0.54).
Hemoglobin shows no marginal association with PFS (r=-0.005, p=0.30).
Alkaline phosphatase weakly negatively correlated with PFS (r=-0.013, p=0.003).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment with treatment_tamoxifen prolongs pfs_months overall.
Treatment with treatment_palbociclib prolongs pfs_months overall.
Treatment with treatment_trastuzumab prolongs pfs_months overall.
Treatment with treatment_olaparib prolongs pfs_months overall.
Treatment with treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan prolongs pfs_months overall.
Treatment with treatment_pembrolizumab prolongs pfs_months overall.

analyses.result_summary: Tamoxifen +0.043 mo PFS (4.72 vs 4.68); not significant (p=0.077).
Palbociclib +1.10 mo PFS (5.41 vs 4.31), highly significant (p<1e-300). Largest unconditional treatment effect.
Trastuzumab -0.061 mo PFS (4.65 vs 4.71), small negative (p=0.029).
Olaparib -0.036 mo PFS (4.66 vs 4.70), not significant (p=0.33).
Sacituzumab-govitecan -0.007 mo PFS (4.69 vs 4.69), null (p=0.85).
Pembrolizumab -0.074 mo PFS (4.63 vs 4.70), small negative (p=0.018).

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_tamoxifen prolongs pfs_months specifically in er_positive=1 patients (positive treatment-by-ER interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Stratified: in ER+, tamoxifen +0.023 mo (p=0.45); in ER-, tamoxifen +0.081 mo (p=0.030). Interaction coef -0.009 (p=0.77). No evidence of an ER-restricted tamoxifen benefit; if anything, the larger raw effect is in ER- (small).

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_palbociclib prolongs pfs_months specifically in HR+/HER2- (er_positive=1 OR pr_positive=1) AND her2_positive=0 patients (positive treatment-by-subtype interaction).

analyses.result_summary: In HR+/HER2- (n=30444): palbo +1.81 mo (5.46 vs 4.31, p<1e-300). Outside HR+/HER2- (n=19556): palbo -0.009 mo (p=0.78). Interaction coef +1.82 (p<1e-300). Huge subgroup-specific effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_trastuzumab prolongs pfs_months specifically in her2_positive=1 patients (positive treatment-by-HER2 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: In HER2+ (n=9060): trastuzumab -0.079 mo (p=0.15). In HER2- (n=40940): trastuzumab -0.051 mo (p=0.10). Interaction coef +0.012 (p=0.78). NO evidence of HER2+-restricted trastuzumab benefit; effect is null/slightly negative across the board.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_olaparib prolongs pfs_months specifically in patients with any BRCA mutation (brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1) — positive treatment-by-BRCA interaction.

analyses.result_summary: In BRCA-mut (n=2499, 239 treated): olaparib +0.346 mo (4.93 vs 4.59), p=0.041. In BRCA-wt (n=47501): olaparib -0.056 mo, p=0.14. Interaction coef +0.140 (p=0.16). Marginal positive direction in BRCA mutated; effect modest in absolute terms.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab prolongs pfs_months specifically in TNBC patients (er_positive=0 AND pr_positive=0 AND her2_positive=0) — positive treatment-by-TNBC interaction.

analyses.result_summary: In TNBC (n=10496, 1578 treated): pembro +0.072 mo (4.82 vs 4.75), p=0.22. In non-TNBC (n=39504): pembro -0.114 mo (p=0.0017). Direction reverses by subtype (positive trend in TNBC), but effect not significant in TNBC.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan prolongs pfs_months specifically in TNBC patients.

analyses.result_summary: In TNBC: SG -0.085 mo (p=0.22). In non-TNBC: SG +0.019 mo (p=0.66). No TNBC-restricted benefit signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for demographics, disease severity, biomarkers, and labs, treatment_palbociclib remains the only treatment with a robust positive main effect on pfs_months; trastuzumab, olaparib, pembrolizumab, and tamoxifen show no positive adjusted main effect.

analyses.result_summary: R²=0.792. Adjusted treatment coefficients: palbociclib +1.101 (p<1e-300), tamoxifen -0.008 (p=0.48), trastuzumab -0.009 (p=0.47), olaparib -0.026 (p=0.12), sacituzumab +0.033 (p=0.049), pembrolizumab -0.020 (p=0.17). Strong adjusted predictors of PFS: age +0.176/yr, ECOG -1.17/level, stage_iv -1.55, brain_mets -0.98, ER+ +0.53, HER2+ -0.47, PIK3CA -0.58, albumin +0.48/g/dL, weight_loss -0.077/%, ki67 -0.019/%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The palbociclib benefit is concentrated specifically in er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 patients (regardless of pr_positive), with essentially no benefit in any other ER/HER2 cell.

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests across all 8 ER×PR×HER2 cells
analyses.result_summary: Stratified: ER+/HER2- cells: palbo +1.92 mo (E1P0H0 +1.77, E1P1H0 +1.95). ER+/HER2+ cells: palbo +0.02 mo. ER-/HER2- cells: palbo -0.004 mo. ER-/HER2+ cells: palbo -0.09 mo. Effect requires both ER+ and HER2-; PR status irrelevant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Systematic screen of treatment-by-binary-feature interactions (adjusted) will reveal modifier patterns: palbociclib×PIK3CA negative, palbociclib×HER2_low positive, sacituzumab×brain_mets positive, pembrolizumab×ER negative, plus expected palbociclib×ER positive and palbociclib×HER2 negative.

analyses.code: For each treatment × each binary feature: smf.ols('pfs ~ tx*feat + adj'); record interaction p
analyses.result_summary: Palbociclib interactions: ER+ +1.56 (p<1e-300), PR+ +1.03 (p<1e-300), HER2+ -1.34 (p<1e-300), HER2-low +0.38 (p=3e-67), PIK3CA-mut -1.67 (p<1e-300). Sacituzumab×brain_mets +0.195 (p=0.002). Pembrolizumab×ER+ -0.075 (p=0.028). Trastuzumab, olaparib, tamoxifen: no significant binary modifiers identified at p<0.01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ER+/HER2- patients, treatment_palbociclib prolongs pfs_months specifically in those with pik3ca_mutation=0 (PIK3CA wildtype); the benefit is suppressed in pik3ca_mutation=1.

analyses.result_summary: ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt (n=18681, 6571 treated): palbo +2.91 mo (7.21 vs 4.30), p=0. ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-mut (n=9908, 3400 treated): palbo +0.02 mo (4.36 vs 4.34), p=0.62. 3-way interaction palbo:ER:PIK3CA in HER2- subset: coef -2.91 (p=0). PIK3CA mutation completely abolishes palbociclib benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan prolongs pfs_months specifically in patients with has_brain_mets=1 (positive treatment-by-brain-mets interaction).

analyses.code: Stratified t-test by brain_mets; interaction in adjusted model
analyses.result_summary: In brain_mets+ (n=5090, 490 treated): SG +0.162 mo (3.90 vs 3.73), p=0.18 (unadjusted t-test). In brain_mets- (n=44910): SG -0.030 mo, p=0.44. Interaction coef +0.173 (p=0.009). Adjusted within brain_mets: SG +0.195 mo (p=0.001).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the palbociclib-responsive subgroup, the benefit is larger when ki67_pct is lower (negative treatment-by-Ki67 interaction).
Pembrolizumab benefit is modified by inflammatory/disease-burden continuous markers (e.g., ki67_pct, weight_loss_pct_6mo, crp_mg_l, nlr).

analyses.result_summary: Palbociclib×ki67_pct interaction coef -0.053 (p<1e-300). Each 1% increase in Ki67 reduces palbociclib benefit by ~0.05 mo. Confirms benefit larger in low-proliferation tumors.

Among continuous moderators of pembrolizumab effect, only sacituzumab×crp showed p<0.05 in the broader screen; pembro×continuous markers were not significant overall. In TNBC subset, pembrolizumab×weight_loss_pct_6mo coef +0.0087 (p=0.004) — pembrolizumab benefit larger in cachectic TNBC patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutated patients is concentrated in HER2-negative disease (positive ola×BRCA in HER2- subset).
Olaparib benefit in BRCA-mutated patients is concentrated specifically in er_positive=0 (ER-negative) disease.

analyses.result_summary: BRCA-mut/HER2- (n=2023, 193 treated): olaparib +0.32 mo (4.96 vs 4.64), p=0.09. BRCA-mut/HER2+ (n=476, 46 treated): +0.46 mo, p=0.25 (small n). BRCA-wt/HER2-: olaparib -0.03 mo (p=0.47). Trend persists in HER2-/BRCA+ but not significant individually.
BRCA-mut/ER- (n=750, 55 treated): olaparib +0.83 mo (5.01 vs 4.18), p=0.0012. BRCA-mut/ER+ (n=1749, 184 treated): olaparib +0.14 mo, p=0.49. Strongest unadjusted signal is BRCA-mut + ER- (TNBC-like).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within TNBC, pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in premenopausal patients (postmenopausal=0).
Within TNBC, pembrolizumab benefit is modified by stage_iv status.

analyses.result_summary: TNBC/premeno (n=4163, 611 treated): pembro +0.275 mo (4.50 vs 4.23), p=0.003. TNBC/postmeno (n=6333, 967 treated): pembro -0.059 mo, p=0.43. Non-TNBC subgroups uniformly show negative pembro effect. Suggests TNBC + premenopausal pocket of benefit.
TNBC/stage I-III: pembro +0.070 mo (p=0.28). TNBC/stage IV: pembro -0.061 mo (p=0.56). No clear stage modifier in TNBC.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Trastuzumab has no detectable PFS benefit in any biologically reasonable HER2+ subgroup (e.g., HER2+/ER+, HER2+/ER-, HER2+/non-stage-IV, HER2+/no brain mets).

analyses.code: Stratified t-tests within HER2+ by ER, stage, brain mets, BRCA, PIK3CA
analyses.result_summary: HER2+/ER+: tras -0.087 mo (p=0.18). HER2+/ER-: tras -0.063 mo (p=0.55). HER2+/stage_iv=0: -0.042 (p=0.50). HER2+/stage_iv=1: -0.108 (p=0.24). No subgroup of HER2+ disease shows significant trastuzumab benefit. Adjusted effect in HER2+: +0.011 mo (p=0.27).

proposed_hypotheses.text: An exhaustive 1-3 binary feature subgroup search will confirm that palbociclib's strongest subgroups all match ER+ AND HER2- AND PIK3CA-wt; no non-palbociclib treatment will yield a multi-feature subgroup with effect >0.5 mo at p<0.001.

analyses.code: Enumerate all combinations of up to 3 binary features and binary values; compute t-test for each treatment
analyses.result_summary: Palbociclib top subgroups all share ER+, HER2-, PIK3CA-wt (effects +2.4 to +2.9 mo, all p<1e-200). Pembrolizumab, sacituzumab, tamoxifen: NO subgroups passed |diff|>0.2 AND p<0.001 thresholds. Olaparib: only NEGATIVE subgroups in HER2+/node-negative (likely chance/confounding). Trastuzumab: only one negative subgroup (HER2-low/stage I-III/node+). Confirms palbociclib is the only treatment with a strong, replicated subgroup-restricted positive effect at this scale.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for age, ECOG, stage, brain mets, albumin, ki67, the apparent pembrolizumab benefit in TNBC + premenopausal disappears (i.e., it is confounded by patient/disease factors).

analyses.code: smf.ols pfs ~ pembrolizumab + age + ecog + stage + brain + albumin + ki67 within TNBC/premeno
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted pembro coef in TNBC/premeno: +0.021 mo (p=0.16). 3-way interaction pembro:tnbc:postmenopausal: coef -0.016 (p=0.85). The unadjusted +0.275 mo TNBC/premeno effect attenuates to ~0 after age/ECOG adjustment. No robust pembrolizumab subgroup detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment, the BRCA-mutated olaparib benefit disappears (confounded by other patient/disease factors).
Olaparib benefit is concentrated in BRCA-mut + node_positive=1 patients (this combination showed +0.74 mo unadjusted).

analyses.code: smf.ols pfs ~ olaparib + age + ecog + stage + brain + er + her2 + albumin + ki67 within BRCA-mut
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted olaparib in BRCA-mut: coef +0.024 (p=0.78). Adjusted in BRCA-mut/HER2-: +0.027 (p=0.80). Adjusted in BRCA-mut/ER- (TNBC-like): +0.033 (p=0.54). Adjusted ola×BRCA full-data interaction with rich covariates: +0.045 (p=0.61). Unadjusted BRCA-mut signal is largely confounding.

analyses.code: smf.ols pfs ~ olaparib + adj within BRCA-mut & node+
analyses.result_summary: BRCA-mut/node+ (n=1281, 126 treated): unadjusted +0.74 mo (p=0.003); adjusted +0.094 mo (p=0.43). Effect not robust to adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within brain-mets+ patients, the sacituzumab benefit is concentrated in er_positive=1 disease (TNBC/brain-mets patients do not benefit).

analyses.code: Stratified within brain_mets+ by ER and TNBC; adjusted within brain_mets/ER+
analyses.result_summary: Brain+/ER+ (n=3581, 324 treated): SG +0.318 mo (4.22 vs 3.90), p=0.044; adjusted +0.236 mo, p=0.008. Brain+/ER- (n=1509, 166 treated): SG -0.065 mo, p=0.71. Brain+/HER2+ (separately): trend +0.30 (p=0.04). Brain+/non-TNBC: SG +0.30 mo, p=0.038. Brain+/TNBC: SG -0.19 mo, p=0.35. Sacituzumab benefit in brain mets is HR+/HER2-positive driven; ER- brain-mets patients do not benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL: treatment_palbociclib produces a large positive effect on pfs_months (~+2.9 months, fully adjusted) restricted to the subgroup defined by er_positive=1 AND her2_positive=0 AND pik3ca_mutation=0 (ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wildtype). PIK3CA mutation completely suppresses the benefit; HER2-positivity also suppresses; ER-negativity also suppresses.

FINAL: treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan produces a small positive effect on pfs_months (~+0.20 months, adjusted) restricted to the subgroup defined by has_brain_mets=1; the effect within brain mets is concentrated in er_positive=1 patients (er_positive=0 brain-mets patients do not benefit).

FINAL: treatment_pembrolizumab shows no robust adjusted PFS benefit in any subgroup; an unadjusted positive trend in TNBC + premenopausal (er_positive=0, pr_positive=0, her2_positive=0, postmenopausal=0) attenuates to null after age/ECOG adjustment.

FINAL: treatment_olaparib shows no robust adjusted PFS benefit; an unadjusted positive trend in BRCA-mutated (brca1_mutation=1 OR brca2_mutation=1) patients (+0.35 months, p=0.04) and in BRCA-mut/ER- subset (+0.83 months, p=0.001 unadjusted) attenuates to null after multivariable adjustment.
FINAL: treatment_trastuzumab shows no PFS benefit in any subgroup including her2_positive=1 (adjusted coef +0.01 mo, p=0.27). Effect is null/slightly negative everywhere.

FINAL: treatment_tamoxifen shows no clinically meaningful PFS effect overall (+0.04 mo, p=0.08) and no significant interaction with er_positive, postmenopausal status, or any continuous biomarker. Adjusted effect in ER+ subset is null (-0.004 mo, p=0.84).

analyses.code: smf.ols pfs ~ palbociclib + adj within ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt (n=18681)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted palbociclib coef in ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-wt: +2.921 mo (p<1e-300, n_treated=6571). Same model in ER+/HER2-/PIK3CA-mut: +0.004 mo (p=0.25, n_treated=3400). Effect is consistent across all sub-strata of this subgroup (postmenopausal, sex, node+, stage IV, brain mets, BRCA): all show +2.55 to +3.04 months, p<1e-29.

analyses.code: smf.ols pfs ~ sacituzumab + adj within has_brain_mets=1; subset by ER
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted sacituzumab in brain_mets+ (n=5090, 490 treated): +0.179 mo (p=0.004). Within brain_mets+/ER+ (n=3581): +0.236 mo (p=0.008); within brain_mets+/ER+/HER2-: +0.259 mo (p=0.015). Sacituz×brain_mets interaction in full data: +0.173 (p=0.009).

analyses.code: smf.ols pfs ~ pembrolizumab + adj within TNBC/premeno; full-data 3-way interaction
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted pembrolizumab in TNBC/premenopausal (n=4163, 611 treated): +0.021 mo (p=0.16). In TNBC overall: +0.007 mo (p=0.50). Pembro:tnbc:postmenopausal 3-way: -0.016 (p=0.85). No robust adjusted subgroup signal.

analyses.code: smf.ols pfs ~ olaparib + adj within BRCA-mut; ola×BRCA adjusted
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted olaparib in BRCA-mut (n=2499, 239 treated): +0.024 mo (p=0.78). Unadjusted in BRCA-mut/ER-: +0.83 mo (p=0.001) but adjusted +0.033 (p=0.54). Adjusted ola×BRCA interaction: +0.045 (p=0.61). No robust adjusted subgroup signal.

analyses.code: smf.ols pfs ~ trastuzumab + adj within HER2+
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted trastuzumab in HER2+ (n=9060, 1890 treated): +0.011 mo (p=0.27). All HER2+ sub-strata null. No subgroup of HER2+ disease shows trastuzumab benefit.

analyses.code: smf.ols pfs ~ tamoxifen + adj within ER+; tam×postmenopausal interaction
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted tamoxifen in ER+ (n=34886, 10535 treated): -0.004 mo (p=0.84). Tam×postmenopausal interaction: coef -0.009 (p=0.75). Tam×continuous moderators: none significant in ER+. No robust subgroup signal.
